topic: Amp Code Review 2026: Sourcegraph's Autonomous Coding Agent Tested

competitors.title: Amp Code AI Review 2026: Autonomous Agent for Developers | Second Talent
key_points: Multi-model routing across Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 3, and GPT-5
Parallel subagents for refactors across many files at once
Unconstrained token usage with no hard caps on output quality
Free ad-supported tier plus Smart Mode with zero data sharing
Thread sharing and team leaderboards for reusable workflows
Dual interface: VS Code extension or standalone CLI
Oracle Mode for architecture reviews and design suggestions
Amp Tab: Free autocomplete engine that knows recent changes and compiler errors
Handoff System replaces compaction - cleanly transfer context between threads without lossy summaries
Custom Commands via Markdown files or executables in .agents/commands/
AGENTS.md support for project-specific build steps, test commands, and common pitfalls
Functions as a semi-autonomous collaborator rather than simple assistant
Uses specialized subagents: UI Agent (Claude Opus 4.5), Code Agent (Gemini 3), Reasoning Agent (GPT-5)

stats.fact: Amp Code uses Claude Opus 4.5 for UI/design tasks, Gemini 3 for code generation, and GPT-5 for natural language interpretation
Supports up to 200k token context window
Free tier is ad-supported with no training opt-in required
Smart Mode (paid tier) offers zero data sharing for proprietary/client code

angles: Amp Code is Sourcegraph's autonomous coding agent that runs multi-step tasks across your repo
Agentic workflows vs traditional AI coding assistants
Team collaboration features: thread sharing, public profiles, leaderboards
Enterprise-ready with zero data sharing options
Parallel subagents architecture enables simultaneous multi-file refactors
Comparison against competitors: Claude Code for terminal-first developers, Cursor for GUI-centric workflows
